scenario_summary: An enterprise host on the LTS channel needs to roll back from a faulty LTS cut to the channel's LTS floor. The downgrade is admissible because channel.lts declares downgrade_supported_to_lts_floor and last_known_good_repair_path_class rollback_to_lts_floor; the admin or managed-fleet updater authorises the move because LTS rows MUST carry an admin or managed updater per the install-topology matrix. The configuration root, recovery root, derived cache root, and admin-policy root all stay under their lts-suffixed owning channel.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: The administrator has rolled this LTS install back to the channel's LTS floor. Your settings, recovery state, and managed policy stay in place because every LTS state root is channel-suffixed.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: LTS supports downgrade to the channel LTS floor with admin or managed updater authority. Crossing the LTS floor without a release-council waiver is not supported.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support exports cite the LTS floor as the named rollback target, the admin-policy root as the update-marker owner, and the admin or managed updater as the actor. No cross-channel state migration is implied.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Promote with admin authority. The downgrade target is the named LTS floor, not a free-text version string; the install-topology matrix's rollback_target_class channel_lts_floor is the supporting evidence.

notes: Reference fixture for the downgrade_supported_to_lts_floor downgrade posture and the rollback_to_lts_floor repair path.
